content.problemStatement:
You are given a square matrix MMM with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN). Initially, all the elements of this matrix are equal to AAA. The matrix is broken down in NNN steps (numbered 111 through NNN); note that during this process, some elements of the matrix are simply marked as removed, but all elements are still indexed in the same way as in the original matrix. For each valid iii, the iii-th step consists of the following:


Elements M1,N−i+1,M2,N−i+1,…,Mi−1,N−i+1M_{1, N-i+1}, M_{2, N-i+1}, \ldots, M_{i-1, N-i+1}M1,N−i+1​,M2,N−i+1​,…,Mi−1,N−i+1​ are removed.
Elements Mi,N−i+1,Mi,N−i+2,…,Mi,NM_{i, N-i+1}, M_{i, N-i+2}, \ldots, M_{i, N}Mi,N−i+1​,Mi,N−i+2​,…,Mi,N​ are removed.
Let's denote the product of all 2i−12i-12i−1 elements removed in this step by pip_ipi​. Each of the remaining elements of the matrix (those which have not been removed yet) is multiplied by pip_ipi​.
Find the sum p1+p2+p3+…+pNp_1 + p_2 + p_3 + \ldots + p_Np1​+p2​+p3​+…+pN​. Since this number could be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and AAA.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the sum of products at each step modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
